name: Norman Simmons
abstract: Norman Simmons (October 6, 1929 – May 13, 2021) was an American musician, arranger, composer, educator, and most prominently a pianist who worked extensively with Helen Humes, Carmen McRae, Sarah Vaughan, Anita O'Day, and Joe Williams among others.

quotes.text: Ellis. Joe had been using Ellis in New York. And Ellis had gotten his permanent gig, the guy opened the Carnegie Tavern right behind Carnegie Hall and gave Ellis this gig as a house — that was his gig. So that’s how I happened to get the call and go in there. And so since Paul couldn’t travel, and I’d known John from Chicago too originally. And he’s always been in my corner. And so he said, “get Norman to do it.” So actually when Joe called me he didn’t go through any official hiring. He called me and said, “John Levy’s going to send you the itinerary, and you pick out what you want to do.” That was his way of saying the job was mine.
quotes.caption: Norman Simmons recalls joining Joe Williams' band
quotes.doc_title: Norman Simmons interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 27, 2007 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: I went in there with the trio, with Norman Simmons and -- well, it's a quartet -- Norman Simmons and Eddie Locke and [Vic] Sproles Vic Sproles, a bass player.

about_quotes.speaker: Mat Domber
about_quotes.evidence: I mean we have people like Norman Simmons who play regularly with us
about_quotes.doc_title: Mat Domber interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Etta Jones
about_quotes.evidence: And Norman Simmons plays on it? ... Norman Simmons, yeah. Houston got the group together... he wants certain musicians he thinks would be best for that type of music I’m doing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Etta Jones interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 2, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: So we kept working with the Basie band... we just take his own, Norman Simmons, his pianist, and then work within the context of the Basie band.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy interviewed by Monk Rowe, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: This is how we hit upon a group with Norman Simmons, Sweets Edison, and different people that he put together for his group.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jillean Williams
about_quotes.evidence: Norman, who, Norman Simmons booked a drummer... Norman thought he showed great promise and so he booked him for this job.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jillean Williams interviewed by Milt Fillius and Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 20, 1995
